document: Scope 3 Borg SWE-bench Pre-Registered Decision Rules
owner: Hermes subagent on behalf of AB

primary_confirmatory_family.note: 6-member confirmatory family replaces the brief's proposed family=20. Family=20 anti-pattern is rejected because it inflates correction onto descriptive reads. See STATS_PLAN.md §4.6.

descriptive_family.note: Results reported as 'descriptively supportive' or 'descriptively null'. No confirmatory claim drawn.

exploratory_family.note: Pre-registered directional hypothesis only. All p-values reported with [exploratory, uncorrected] tag.

primary_test: pooled mixed-effects GLMM (clustered on task, fixed effects on framework, interaction term on condition x framework)
fallback_test: cluster-robust GEE if GLMM fails to converge
sensitivity_test: Bayesian GLMM (statsmodels BinomialBayesMixedGLM) reported alongside frequentist fit

rules.description: Borg scaffold helps overall (C1 vs C0, pooled across frameworks)
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Borg scaffold improves agent success pooled across agent frameworks.

rules.description: Borg seeded knowledge adds value over scaffold (C2 vs C1, pooled, collective knowledge)
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Collective knowledge (seeded DB) adds value beyond the scaffold alone.
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fails: Scaffold-only effect cannot be distinguished from full treatment. Report effect CI and descriptively note the null.

rules.description: Borg total product effect (C2 vs C0, pooled — primary headline)
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Borg (full product) improves agent success vs no-borg baseline pooled across frameworks.
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fails: No evidence borg improves overall success (R0A).

rules.contrast: condition x framework (6 df full, 4 df in fallback)

interpretation_matrix.interaction_fail_to_reject_AND_R3_fires: Borg's benefit generalizes across the tested LLM loops (no detectable framework-specific interaction).
interpretation_matrix.interaction_rejects_AND_signs_disagree: Borg is framework-specific. Effect direction depends on agent loop.
interpretation_matrix.interaction_rejects_AND_signs_agree: Borg helps all tested frameworks but effect size varies. Report per-framework CIs.
interpretation_matrix.interaction_fail_to_reject_AND_R3_fails: No evidence borg helps any framework. This is null, not generalization.

rules.caveat: Power for this test is ~0.20-0.30 at OR_ratio=2.0. A non-rejection is NOT strong evidence of generalization.

rules.description: Hermes-family vs OpenClaw (1-df contrast, Q4 — AB's key question)
rules.contrast: condition x framework_family (framework_family in {hermes_like, openclaw}, 1 df)

interpretation_matrix.contrast_fail_to_reject_AND_R3_fires: Borg's benefit generalizes from Hermes-style loops to OpenClaw at the loop-family level.
interpretation_matrix.contrast_rejects_AND_both_positive: Both framework families benefit but at different magnitudes.
interpretation_matrix.contrast_rejects_AND_one_null: Borg helps one framework family but not the other. Product is framework-coupled.
interpretation_matrix.contrast_fail_to_reject_AND_R3_fails: No evidence borg helps; family contrast uninterpretable.

rules.conditional_on: R_OPENCLAW_UNAVAILABLE is not triggered. Otherwise R5 is skipped and Q4 marked 'not answered in this experiment'.
rules.caveat: Framework families are not apples-to-apples. See Red Team finding (l). A non-rejection may reflect framework-level confounds (tool schema, prompts, model) rather than true borg invariance.

rules.conditional_on: R3 fires (borg must help at all before cost question is meaningful)
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Borg is net cost-effective at current prices.
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fails_but_R3_fires: Borg improves success at the cost of increased tokens. Net cost-effectiveness depends on user price/quality trade-off.
rules.note: Added by Red Team finding (m). Required to answer the product question 'is borg worth paying for'.

rules.description: Null: no evidence borg helps overall
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: No evidence borg helps pooled across frameworks. Pre-registered null reported.
rules.caveat: A null at this N reflects the MDE OR ~2.99; effects smaller than that are not ruled out.

rules.description: Descriptive per-framework null (for each framework F)
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Descriptively, no evidence borg helps framework F at N=15. Report 95% CI for per-framework OR(C2-C0|F).
rules.caveat: Per-framework N=15 has power below 0.30 at OR=3.0. Null is uninformative.

rules.contrast: (C2_transfer | other_agent_seeded_DB) vs (C1_empty | empty_DB) on matched task-pairs
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Preliminary evidence of cross-agent knowledge transfer (EXPLORATORY, N=10, power <= 0.20). Requires replication at N >= 40 for confirmatory claim.
rules.note: This rule does NOT carry a confirmatory product claim. Reported as exploratory evidence only.

rules.description: Phase C null: collective intelligence NOT supported
rules.test: Phase C McNemar failed or direction negative or completion < 80%
rules.conclusion_if_rule_fires: Phase C does not support the cross-agent knowledge-transfer claim. Given power <= 0.20, this is not a strong null -- the design cannot rule out a positive effect, only that it was not observed in this sample. The collective-intelligence claim is NOT supported by the current evidence; borg should be positioned as a scaffold, not a memory, until replication at N >= 40 finds otherwise.
rules.note: Honest null is a valid outcome. Do not re-label as 'trending toward significance'.

rules.trigger: any C1 or C2 cell reports borg_searches == 0
rules.action: Re-run cell once. If re-run also reports 0, HALT experiment, investigate cause, do not resume until fixed.

rules.trigger: fewer than 30% of borg_search calls in C1/C2 are 'influential' (output text referenced in next 5 tool calls)
rules.action: Cell flagged. If > 30% of C2 cells are flagged, the C2 confirmatory claim is downgraded with the caveat 'the treatment was nominally applied but appears to have been mostly ignored'.

rules.trigger: cell fails due to OOM, Docker daemon death, network timeout to LLM API, agent loop harness bug
rules.action: Drop from paired analysis, track in crash bucket. If crash bucket > 10% of total runs, pause experiment for investigation.

rules.trigger: preflight B subagent reports OpenClaw is not runnable, or pilot integrity checks fail on OpenClaw
rules.action: Drop to Scope 3- (3 frameworks). Phase C drops to 2 seed-eval framework families (6 pairs). Rerun power analysis with N_fw=3. R5 skipped; Q4 marked 'not answered in this experiment'.

rules.trigger: first 3 pilot runs of Phase B on Sonnet show C0 baseline outside [0.20, 0.75]
rules.action: Pause Phase B, swap tasks to restore 40-60% baseline, document.

rules.trigger: post-hoc scan of agent run logs reveals diff-like content in hints_text that slipped past filter, OR Phase A trace contains > 30 n-gram overlap with any task's hints_text
rules.action: Affected tasks marked contaminated. GLMM re-fit with those tasks dropped. Both versions reported.

rules.description: Phase A seeding quality gate (Red Team (p))
rules.trigger: manual inspection of 5 random traces from Phase A DB reveals any of: empty traces, boilerplate-only content, not retrievable by borg search with relevant queries, fewer than 500 distinct file references or 5000 distinct tokens across all 30 traces
rules.action: HALT before Phase B. Investigate, fix seeding pipeline, re-run Phase A.

Add an opportunistic C0.5 condition on a 5-task subset: borg tools available, empty DB, borg search monkey-patched to return fixed boilerplate. Compare to C1 on those 5 tasks to estimate 'is the empty-response structure itself treatment?' bias.

modifications_required: Promote pooled GLMM to PRIMARY confirmatory analysis (replaces brief's per-framework McNemar as primary)
Downgrade per-framework McNemar and Cochran's Q to descriptive-only (no alpha spend)
Downgrade Phase C to exploratory (no confirmatory claim possible at N=10)
Reduce confirmatory family from 20 tests to 6 (Holm alpha_min = 0.0083 not 0.0025)
Add R6 cost-effectiveness rule to confirmatory family
Add R_THEATER_DOWNGRADE rule and response-influence post-hoc diagnostic (Red (e))
Add R_C0_5_BOILERPLATE_CONTROL for C1/C0 prompt-confound mitigation (Red (a))
Pre-commit Phase A / Phase B file-overlap maximum (Red (b))
Phase A seeding agents given redacted tasks (hints_text empty) (Red (f))
Always fit both random-intercept and random-slope GLMM; use larger SE (Red (j))
Pre-commit Phase C task-pair tuples at lock-in (Red (k))
Pre-publish OpenClaw adapter code; report 'delta from own baseline' framing (Red (l))
Git-hash the pre-registration and cite in final report (Red (h))

recommendation_if_AB_wants_per_framework.note: At N=60 per framework, McNemar Holm-corrected power reaches ~0.60 at OR=3.0. True confirmatory per-framework claims require ~N=100.
